name: Fontainebleau State Park
summary_text: A beautiful Louisiana State Park on the north shore of Lake Pontchartrain offering shaded campsites, waterfront scenery, historic ruins, and easy access to New Orleans.

siteFeatures.level: Mostly level paved campsites beneath mature trees.
otherNotes: Large mature trees provide abundant shade.
Several campsites are within walking distance of Lake Pontchartrain.
A peaceful setting despite being close to New Orleans.

starlink.notes: Heavy tree cover throughout the campground makes Starlink difficult.

petNotes.walkingAreas: Excellent paved roads and shaded trails make this a wonderful park for walking dogs.
petNotes.dogFriendlyTrails: Leashed pets are allowed on most park trails.
petNotes.petRestrictions: Pets must remain leashed at all times.
petNotes.hazards: Mosquitoes can be heavy, especially during warmer months. Watch for snakes in natural areas.
petNotes.notes: The shaded campground and miles of paved walking areas make this one of the better state parks for traveling with dogs.

tripTips: An excellent basecamp for visiting New Orleans without staying in the city.
Walk or bike along the Lake Pontchartrain shoreline.
Walk out to the Lake Pontchartrain shoreline, especially around sunset.
Take an airboat or swamp tour while you're here—it's one of the most memorable experiences in the area.
Visit the historic sugar mill ruins inside the park.
Reserve early during spring and fall weekends.
Bring bug spray during warmer months.
